candidates.title: Guinea fowl sales spike in N.S. as homeowners look for natural tick control
candidates.description: Some guinea fowl breeders in Nova Scotia are seeing a surge in demand for the birds to control the tick population. Scientists note they could serve as a supplementary control method, and breeders stress space requirements.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Federal agency touts operation of cybersecurity sensors across the North
candidates.description: The Communications Security Establishment's latest annual report notes that its Cyber Centre deployed the software on government networks in the Northwest Territories in 2022 followed by Yukon and Nunavut in 2024.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: How Cape Race, N.L., helped North America get its news — by fishing it out of the sea
candidates.description: Before there was internet, television and radio, Cape Race, N.L., was at the centre of how North America got its news. Theresa O'Leary's book Race to the Cape tells us how, and why it started here.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Rare tick-bite allergy leaves Nova Scotia woman unable to eat meat or dairy
candidates.description: A Pictou County woman says she is afraid to leave her house after being diagnosed with alpha-gal syndrome, a rare allergic reaction to meat and dairy linked to lone star tick bites.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Staff fear rot and anti-'rat' culture help fuel escape risks at Port Coquitlam jail
candidates.description: A jail that has held the likes of serial killer Robert Pickton, war criminals and Canada’s most notorious gangsters awaiting trials in its segregation cells is in an internal spiral.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: B.C. drowning deaths rise sharply as safety groups urge life-jacket use
candidates.description: B.C. has recorded the sharpest increase in drowning deaths, higher than any other provinces in Canada this year, according to the B.C. and Yukon Lifesaving Society. It says at least 30 people have drowned in the province so far in 2026, a 50 per cent increase from the same point last year.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Man who took Hamilton bus on joy ride accepts longer jail sentence to get mental health treatment
candidates.description: The man who took a Hamilton city bus on a joy ride — with passengers still on board — is serving nine-months in jail. Though the Crown asked for a lower sentence, the man agreed to serve an extra month so he could get into a Brockville, Ont., treatment facility the judge recommended.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: A ship exploded in Lake Huron 143 years ago. Now, a team is trying to find its wreckage
candidates.description: A group of marine archaeologists and volunteers will take to Boiler Beach near Kincardine, Ont., this summer to learn more about the SS Erie Belle steamboat explosion from 1883.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: B.C.'s Highway 1 reopens in Fraser Canyon, but twin wildfires remain out of control
candidates.description: Officials reopened Highway 1 through British Columbia's Fraser Canyon on Saturday, but warned that active wildfire operations continue in the area.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: N.L. musician Mark Hiscock's death from sepsis was preventable, says his family
candidates.description: More than a year after Newfoundland musician Mark Hiscock died unexpectedly from sepsis, his family is still reeling from the loss — and say his death was preventable.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: What we know about the deadly charter boat sinking off B.C.’s coast
candidates.description: Here’s what we know about the deadly charter boat sinking off the shores of Richmond, B.C., the people who were aboard, concerns raised about the vessel and the investigation into the company that operated it.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Quebec's population is shifting — and not every region will grow the same way
candidates.description: New projections from the Institut de la statistique du Québec (ISQ) suggest population growth will become increasingly uneven across the province. Parts of Quebec could see their populations shrink over the coming decades, while housing demands continue to grow.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada's next defence fight won’t be over spending. It’ll be over trust
candidates.description: Britain’s political upheaval exposed a simple reality: Defence spending without fiscal credibility is a political liability. Canada has bought itself time by revealing few details of its long-term plans. That changes this fall, when Ottawa must finally show Canadians how its NATO ambitions will be financed.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: First Nations chiefs across country 'blindsided' by pipeline announcements
candidates.description: First Nations chiefs countrywide are expressing concern about the lack of engagement after two pipelines were announced in Alberta, B.C. and Ontario. Two leaders say they only learned of the plans through last-minute emails and rumours.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: $500 cheques to be mailed to flooded Manitobans applying for disaster financial assistance, premier says
candidates.description: A $500 cheque will be in the mail as soon as possible for flood-stricken Manitobans who've applied for disaster financial assistance, and a one-time grant program is in place for anyone in the hardest-hit communities, Manitoba officials said Friday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. Republican Senator Lindsey Graham dead at 71
candidates.description: Lindsey Graham, 71, was a prominent Republican ​senator from South Carolina. Earlier in ​his career, he was a vocal critic of U.S. President Donald Trump ​before becoming one of his most loyal allies on Capitol Hill.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Why the Princess of Wales climbed the highest peaks in England, Scotland and Wales — in a weekend
candidates.description: An outdoor pursuit that took Catherine, Princess of Wales, up peaks in England, Scotland and Wales was both a personal challenge and an opportunity for the House of Windsor to send signals about how a slimmed-down Royal Family approaches its role in trying to be a more relatable 21st-century monarchy.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada's Gabriela Dabrowski return Wimbledon doubles final comes up short
candidates.description: Kristina Mladenovic won a seventh career Grand Slam women's doubles title by teaming up with Guo Hanyu to defeat Canada's Gabriela Dabrowski and Luisa Stefani 6-3, 7-5 in the Wimbledon final Sunday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Venezuela quakes have killed 4,333, injured 16,740, says National Assembly president
candidates.description: The death toll from the two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24 has risen to 4,333, National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez told reporters on Saturday, announcing that the distribution of housing to those affected will begin next week.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Typhoon Bavi makes landfall in China after brushing past Taiwan, belting southern Japan
candidates.description: Typhoon Bavi has made landfall in eastern China, with nearly two million people evacuating ahead of time. The storm has already struck parts of Japan and brushed past Taiwan.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump administration subpoenas New York Times journalists after Air Force One reporting
candidates.description: The Trump administration's move, which seeks to force the reporters to testify before a federal grand jury, should 'shock the conscience' of Americans, a New York Times lawyer says.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Nigel Farage's only by-election challenger is self-proclaimed space alien Count Binface
candidates.description: Nigel Farage, leader of Britain's populist Reform party, says he stepped down from his parliamentary seat so he could trigger a by-election and take on "the establishment." But, so far, his only challenger is a comedian who wears a trash bin on his head.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: South African midfielder Jayden Adams dies at 25, just weeks after playing in World Cup
candidates.description: South African soccer player Jayden Adams, 25, has died just weeks after playing for his country at the FIFA World Cup.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Linda Noskova beats Karolina Muchova in all-Czech Wimbledon final to claim 1st Grand Slam title
candidates.description: Linda Noskova recovered from a second-set meltdown in which she wasted five match points to overcome Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 in an all-Czech final on Saturday for her first Grand Slam trophy.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 6 people dead, dozens wounded as Russia strikes Ukraine with missiles and drones
candidates.description: Fighting continues in Russia's war against Ukraine, which is running low on munitions that would help it shoot down and intercept Russian strikes.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Runner gored in the face at Spain's San Fermin bull run festival
candidates.description: The huge animals knocked bodies to the cobblestones, and stumbling runners caused several pileups during the two-and-a-half-minute run from the pen to the bull ring where bullfighters will kill the bulls later in the day.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump's D.C. reflecting pool drained — again — as renovation troubles continue
candidates.description: Crews are again draining the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool as President Donald Trump’s problem-plagued effort to revamp the waterway pushes well past his initial goal of having it ready by July 4 to mark the nation’s 250th birthday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Apple files lawsuit, accuses OpenAI of stealing trade secrets
candidates.description: Apple on Friday accused OpenAI of stealing trade secrets as it seeks to build its own hardware for ChatGPT, a major rupture in a partnership between the iPhone maker and the artificial intelligence company.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Home exchanges gaining popularity as Canadians look for cheaper ways to travel
candidates.description: With rising travel costs squeezing vacation budgets, more Canadians are trading keys. Global home exchange networks report a massive surge in Canadian members still looking to travel but save big on accommodations.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Meta axes feature that allowed tagging Instagram users to generate AI images of them
candidates.description: Meta killed one feature of its new Muse Image system that would let users tag Instagram accounts and generate images based on an account's profile and posts. Its initial announcement also revealed, to the dismay of some, that Meta AI permissions are automatically turned on for users with public accounts in other countries — including Canada — and must be switched off manually in app settings.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Gordie Howe International Bridge to open July 27, Ottawa confirms
candidates.description: The Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Ontario to Michigan will open July 27, the federal government has confirmed.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Meta data centre in Alberta to start operating before neighbouring power plant
candidates.description: It will be four years before a new natural gas-fired power plant north of Edmonton is operating and able to serve the massive data centre Meta Platforms Inc. is planning nearby. The tech giant expects the $13-billion-plus data centre will be online in the next two to three years, though no exact timeline has been set.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. Justice Dept. reaches deal with South Bow regarding Keystone spill in 2022
candidates.description: The U.S. Justice Department has reached a settlement agreement with pipeline company South Bow Corp. regarding a 2022 rupture of the Keystone pipeline that spilled nearly 13,000 barrels of oil.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran
candidates.description: Iranian forces earlier said the Strait was closed and launched wide-ranging attacks at US allies and bases in the region.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
candidates.description: Donald Trump pays tribute to the 71-year-old South Carolina senator, calling him a "true American patriot" who will be "greatly missed".
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Badly burned British couple rescued from ravine during Spain wildfires, reports say
candidates.description: The unnamed pair were found semi-conscious with 40% burns after being trapped by the blazes raging through Almeria province, local media reports.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
candidates.description: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: T. rex could become most expensive fossil ever - but it's a ​problem for scientists
candidates.description: A 67 million-year-old Tyrannosaurus rex will go on sale in New York with a pre-sale value of $30m.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: At least two killed in Toronto street festival shooting
candidates.description: Police say there was an exchange of gunfire between two individuals at the festival, and two firearms had been recovered from the scene.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Polish PM pledges memorial to victims of WW2 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists'
candidates.description: Warsaw and Kyiv have for decades been at loggerheads over the killings of many thousands of civilians during World War Two.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump administration subpoenas New York Times journalists over Air Force One reporting
candidates.description: The reporters received the legal summons after they reported on alleged security issues with the president's new plane, which was gifted by Qatar.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Adams dies aged 25
candidates.description: South Africa and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25, just weeks after featuring for his country at the World Cup.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: More than 40 kidnapped children and teachers freed after Nigerian army operation
candidates.description: "I feel happy and elated… I feel joy," the head of the local teachers' union tells the BBC.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Indian tourists among 15 killed as speedboat capsizes in Vietnam
candidates.description: Local media say 21 people were saved after the boat overturned in rough seas near an island in south Vietnam.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Ice on testicles and donating blood - the myths sold to men trying to boost their sperm
candidates.description: With men’s fertility increasingly in the spotlight, we meet the men using unproven “hacks” promoted by influencers to up their sperm counts.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: The photo that shows who's really on top in Le Pen's party
candidates.description: Thirty-year-old Jordan Bardella had been groomed for success as France's next hard-right leader. He will now have to wait longer.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Newly-released video shows Peter Falconio's killer questioned
candidates.description: Australian police have released bodycam footage showing one of the last attempts to get Bradley Murdoch to reveal the location of Peter Falconio's body, on the 25th anniversary of his murder.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Helping one of Britain's rarest butterflies
candidates.description: For more than 30 years, Paul Dunn has been working to revive the fortunes of the high brown fritillary.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Toronto police looking for suspects after deadly shooting at festival
candidates.description: At least two people are dead and at least four people are injured after a shooting on Saturday night at a festival celebrating Latin culture in Canada's biggest city.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Alvarez's 112th-minute goal helps lift Argentina past Switzerland 3-1 and into World Cup semifinals
candidates.description: Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. launches fresh strikes on Iran as Tehran says it has closed Strait of Hormuz
candidates.description: The U.S. and Iran have been exchanging strikes, with the U.S. military saying it hit 140 targets in Iran overnight, while Iran said it responded with fire toward Jordan and other Gulf states.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Palestinian aid worker who organized World Cup screenings killed in Israeli strike
candidates.description: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A veteran foreign correspondent looks back on a career covering conflicts
candidates.description: For Reporter's Notebook we hear about what it takes to cover conflict over a decades-long career as a foreign correspondent.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Most people who need glasses don't have them. Can the post office change that?
candidates.description: In some towns in India, a visitor to the post office who's squinting at fine print might be asked: Do you want an eye test?
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Spain edges Belgium and will face France in World Cup semifinal
candidates.description: Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump threatens Iran after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral saw calls for his killing
candidates.description: Trump made the comments on his Truth Social after U.S. officials demanded that Iran make a public statement saying the Strait of Hormuz is open.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: What a Monopoly importer learned when it tried to make things in the U.S.A.
candidates.description: After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Justice Department subpoenas New York Times reporters over Air Force One reporting
candidates.description: The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: What to know about the cyclosporiasis outbreak hitting more than half of U.S. states
candidates.description: A surge of cases of the intestinal illness that causes diarrhea and nausea has been detected in 31 states, according to federal health authorities, but the source is still under investigation.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Influencers are questioning common guidance about breast milk. Here's what science says
candidates.description: The guidance over leftover breast milk says toss it after 2 hours. But a recent preprint study shared on social media has some people questioning that advice.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A promising tale from Senegal of fish, rice .... and dangerous worms
candidates.description: Farmers in Senegal are welcoming fish into their rice paddies. The hope is they'll fertilize the crop, be a source of food ... and eat the snails that carry parasitic worms.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Cyclospora outbreaks grow in the Midwest
candidates.description: Michigan reports 1,250 cases of cyclospora and CDC surveillance shows cases in 17 states. The CDC has initiated a traceback investigation, as the source is not yet known.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 12 at 1:27PM EDT by NWS Melbourne FL
candidates.description: At 127 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm over Ferndale, moving east at 10 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Orlando, Apopka, Altamonte Springs, Ocoee, and Winter Garden.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Advisory issued July 12 at 1:26PM EDT until July 12 at 3:30PM EDT by NWS Jacksonville FL
candidates.description: * WHAT...Urban area and small stream flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected. * WHERE...Southwestern Marion County in northern Florida... * WHEN...Until 330 PM EDT. * IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas. Ponding of water in urban or other areas is occurring or is imminent. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 125 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. This will cause urban and small stream flooding. Between 1 and 2 inches of rain have fallen. - Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 3 inches are expected over the area. This additional rain will result in minor flooding. - Some locations that will experience flooding include... Ocala, Rainbow Lakes Estates, Ocala Airport, Dunnellon, Reddick, Romeo and Lowell. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 12 at 1:26PM EDT by NWS Jacksonville FL
candidates.description: At 126 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm over Flagler Beach, moving east at 5 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts around 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Palm Coast, Flagler Beach, Bunnell, and Beverly Beach.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 12 at 12:25PM CDT by NWS Mobile AL
candidates.description: At 1224 PM CDT, Doppler radar was tracking a cluster of strong thunderstorms near Mobile Regional Airport, or 9 miles northwest of Tillmans Corner, moving east at 10 mph. HAZARD...Winds in excess of 30 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Midtown Mobile, Downtown Mobile, Prichard, Tillmans Corner, Saraland, Theodore, Chickasaw, Satsuma, Semmes, Tanner Williams, I65 And I165, Mobile Regional Airport, Wilmer, I65 And AL 158, and I10 And I65.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 12 at 12:24PM CDT by NWS Amarillo TX
candidates.description: At 1224 PM CDT, Doppler radar was tracking strong thunderstorms along a line extending from 4 miles west of Dawn to 7 miles northwest of Summerfield. Movement was southwest at 5 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts of 50 to 55 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Hereford and Dawn.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 12 at 12:23PM CDT by NWS Shreveport LA
candidates.description: At 1223 PM CDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm over Greenwood, or 9 miles west of Shreveport, moving east at 10 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 30 mph and half inch size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Minor damage to outdoor objects is possible. Locations impacted include... Shreveport, Greenwood, Stonewall, Keithville and Forbing.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Marine Warning issued July 12 at 12:22PM CDT until July 12 at 12:45PM CDT by NWS New Orleans LA
candidates.description: For the following areas... Coastal waters from Port Fourchon Louisiana to Lower Atchafalaya River LA from 20 to 60 NM... Coastal waters from Southwest Pass of the Mississippi River to Port Fourchon Louisiana from 20 to 60 NM... Coastal waters from the Southwest Pass of the Mississippi River to Port Fourchon Louisiana out 20 NM... At 1222 PM CDT, a cluster of severe thunderstorms capable of producing waterspouts were located along a line extending from 20 nm southeast of Louisiana Offshore Oil Port to 27 nm south of Southwest Pass Of the Mississippi River, moving east at 25 knots. HAZARD...Waterspouts and wind gusts 34 knots or greater. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Waterspouts can quickly form and capsize boats...damage vessels and oil rigs...and create suddenly higher waves. Small craft could be damaged in briefly higher winds and suddenly higher waves. A cluster of severe thunderstorms will remain over mainly open waters.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flash Flood Warning issued July 12 at 12:19PM CDT until July 12 at 1:00PM CDT by NWS Louisville KY
candidates.description: FFWLMK The National Weather Service in Louisville has extended the * Flash Flood Warning for... Southeastern Grayson County in central Kentucky... * Until 100 PM CDT. * At 1219 PM CDT, the public reported thunderstorms producing heavy rain across the warned area. Between 2 and 5 inches of rain have fallen. Additional rainfall amounts up to 0.5 inches are possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly. HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms. SOURCE...Public reported. IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses as well as other poor drainage and low-lying areas. * Some locations that will experience flash flooding include... Leitchfield, Johnson Crossroads, Peonia, Skaggstown, Huffman, Meredith, Snap, Rock Creek, Conoloway and Fragrant.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 12 at 1:18PM EDT by NWS Tallahassee FL
candidates.description: At 118 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a cluster of strong thunderstorms near Perry, moving east at 25 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Mayo, Perry, Pinland, Cooks Hammock, Smith, Buckville, Carbur, Alton, Fletcher Springs, Townsend, Hatch Bend, Foley, Salem, Perry-Foley Airport, Bucell Junction, Athena, San Pedro Junction, Hampton Springs, and Fenholloway.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 12 at 12:18PM CDT by NWS Shreveport LA
candidates.description: At 1218 PM CDT, Doppler radar was tracking a line of strong thunderstorms extending from near Diana to near White Oak, or extending from 10 miles southeast of Gilmer to near White Oak, moving east at 10 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 30 mph and pea size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Minor damage to outdoor objects is possible. Locations impacted include... Longview, White Oak, Hallsville, Diana, Harleton and Judson.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: M 6.4 - South Sandwich Islands region
candidates.description: The United States Geological Survey reported an earthquake of magnitude 6.4. The event occurred near South Sandwich Islands region. Official monitoring data may be updated as scientists review additional measurements.
candidates.author: U.S. Geological Survey
